'''Ryan Lee Hall''' (born 27 November 1987) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]], and has played for [[England national rugby league team|England]] and [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] at international level.

Hall began his career with [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]], winning the [[Super League Grand Final]]  in [[2008 Super League Grand Final|2008]], [[2009 Super League Grand Final|2009]], [[2011 Super League Grand Final|2011]], [[2012 Super League Grand Final|2012]], [[2015 Super League Grand Final|2015]] and [[2017 Super League Grand Final|2017]]. In 2014 he was part of the [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] team that won the [[2014 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]] and was the winner of the [[Lance Todd Trophy]]. After leaving Leeds he spent two seasons playing for the [[Sydney Roosters]] in the [[NRL]]. In 2024 he overtook [[Danny McGuire]] to become the [[List of Super League players with 100 or more tries|Super League all-time top try scorer]]. Hall is also the [[England national rugby league team]]'s joint appearance maker and all-time top scorer.

==Early years==
Hall was born in [[Rothwell, West Yorkshire]], England, and attended [[Oulton Academy|Royds School]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Sky Try 9s festivals developing Rugby League in Leeds High Schools |url=https://www.leedsrhinosfoundation.org/sky-try-9s-festivals-developing-rugby-league-in-leeds-high-schools/ |website=Leeds Rhinos Foundation |date=19 May 2016 |access-date=29 August 2023}}</ref>

Hall began playing [[rugby league]] for amateur club [[Oulton Raiders]]. His coach at Oulton contacted the town's professional team, [[Leeds Rhinos]], and Hall subsequently joined the club's Academy in 2006.<ref>{{cite web |title=Ryan Hall can add to his growing reputation for Leeds Rhinos |url=https://www.thetimes.com/sport/rugby-league/article/ryan-hall-can-add-to-his-growing-reputation-for-leeds-rhinos-rckrrfddc6x |website=The Times |access-date=29 August 2023 |date=8 October 2009 |url-access=subscription}}</ref> He made eight appearances for the Junior Academy team in his début season, before quickly being selected for the Senior Academy team. He made eleven appearances for the Senior Academy team & scored three tries, including the Senior Academy Grand Final victory over [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]].{{citation needed|date=July 2025}}

==Playing career==
===Leeds===
====2007–2012====
[[File:Ryan Hall Leeds.jpg|thumb|150px|Hall leaving the pitch at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]] after beating [[Catalans Dragons]] 27–20 in the 2009 Superleague playoff semi final]]
Hall made his [[Super League]] début in May 2007, coming off the bench at the [[Magic Weekend#2007–2008: Cardiff|2007 Millennium Magic weekend]] at the [[Millennium Stadium]] in [[Cardiff]] in the controversial 42–38 victory over [[Bradford Bulls]]. He made his first start a fortnight later in the defeat at [[Hull F.C.]], and scored his first try for the club in July 2007 against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Burrow too good by half as Leeds do the double |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2007/jul/07/rugbyleague.sport |website=The Guardian |access-date=29 August 2023 |date=7 July 2007}}</ref> He went on to make nine first team appearances in 2007, scoring three tries, and was awarded an extended contract at the end of the year.<ref>{{cite web |title=Rhinos show faith in Hall with extended contract |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2007/dec/21/rugbyleague.sport |website=The Guardian |access-date=29 August 2023 |date=21 December 2007}}</ref> He was named in the initial 20-man squad for the [[2007 Super League Grand Final]] against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]],<ref>{{cite web |title=Saints v Rhinos: Teams |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/st-helens-vs-leeds/preview/16621 |website=Sky Sports |access-date=29 August 2023 |date=12 October 2007}}</ref> but was not selected in the final squad.<ref>{{cite news |title=2007 Grand Final |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7041479.stm |website=BBC Sport |date=13 October 2007 |access-date=29 August 2023}}</ref>

In 2008, Hall made 16 appearances and scored nine tries. In July 2008, he suffered a foot injury in Leeds' [[Challenge Cup]] semi-final defeat against St Helens, and was initially ruled out for the rest of the season.<ref>{{cite web |title=Leeds lose Hall and Toopi for season |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2008/jul/29/leedsrhinos.warrington |website=The Guardian |access-date=29 August 2023 |date=29 July 2008}}</ref> However, he recovered from the injury in time to be selected for the [[2008 Super League Grand Final]],<ref>{{cite web |title=Webb's absence deals severe blow to Rhinos' hopes of repeating an Old Trafford upset |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2008/oct/04/leedsrhinos.superleague |website=The Guardian |access-date=29 August 2023 |date=4 October 2008}}</ref> scoring a try in the 24–16 victory, again against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].<ref>{{cite web |title=2008 Grand Final |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7642170.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=29 August 2023 |date=4 October 2008}}</ref> He signed a new three-year contract with the club at the end of the season.<ref>{{cite web |title=Leeds hand winger Hall a new deal |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/leeds/7795592.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=29 August 2023 |date=22 December 2008}}</ref>

In 2009, Hall became a regular in [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds']] first team as the team went on to win their third Grand Final in a row, [[2009 Super League Grand Final|18-10]], again against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Fletcher|first=Paul|title=St Helens 10-18 Leeds Rhinos|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/8290503.stm|access-date=22 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=10 October 2009}}</ref>

Hall ended 2009 as the season's top try-scorer, with 31 tries in just 30 games in all competitions, including five tries in one game in a 76–12 win against [[Castleford Tigers]].<ref>{{cite news |last1=Wilson |first1=Andy |title=Ryan Hall takes five tries in Leeds Rhinos' rout of Castleford Tigers |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2009/aug/14/super-league-leeds-rhinos-castleford-tigers |access-date=23 October 2022 |work=The Guardian |date=14 August 2009}}</ref> He was named Young Player of the Year, as well as runner-up in the Player of the Year award. He also received the [[Rugby League International Federation]]'s Rookie of the Year award for 2009.<ref>{{cite web|access-date=5 December 2013|title=Awards|url=http://www.rlif.com/awards|work=[[Rugby League International Federation]]|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100721195910/http://www.rlif.com/awards|archive-date=21 July 2010|url-status=dead}}</ref>

In 2010, Hall scored 31 tries in 33 appearances & was named in the [[Super League Dream Team]] for the second year in a row and featured in the [[2010 World Club Challenge]] and [[2010 Challenge Cup]] Final for Leeds. He played in the [[2010 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Leeds 6-30 Warrington">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/8938067.stm|title=Leeds 6–30 Warrington|publisher=BBC|date=28 August 2010|access-date=22 November 2019|first=Ged|last=Scott}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/aug/28/leeds-rhinos-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final |location=London |work=The Guardian |title=Chris Hicks hat-trick leads Warrington to Challenge Cup triumph over Leeds |first=Andy |last=Wilson |date=28 August 2010 |access-date=22 November 2019<!--DASHBot--> |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100831105934/http://www.guardian.co.uk/sport/2010/aug/28/leeds-rhinos-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final |archive-date=31 August 2010 |url-status=live}}</ref>

Hall played on the wing and scored two tries for Leeds in the [[2011 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Scott|first=Ged|title=Leeds 18-28 Wigan|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/14670204|access-date=21 November 2019|newspaper=[[BBC Sport]]|date=27 August 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news| url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/aug/27/leeds-rhinos-wigan-warriors-challenge-cup | location=London | work=The Guardian | first=Andy | last=Wilson | title=Leeds Rhinos 18-28 Wigan Warriors – Challenge Cup final match report | date=27 August 2011}}</ref> He also played in the [[2011 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-match-report?newsfeed=true|title=Leeds claim Grand Final glory as inspired Rob Burrow sinks St Helens|date=8 October 2011|work=The Guardian|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/8815944/St-Helens-16-Leeds-32-match-report.html|title=St Helens 16 Leeds 32|date=8 October 2011|work=The Daily Telegraph|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref>

In January 2012, Hall signed a new five-year contract with Leeds.<ref>{{cite web |title=Ryan Hall fends off rugby union interest by extending Leeds contract |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/jan/17/ryan-hall-leeds-rhinos-contract |website=The Guardian |access-date=29 August 2023 |date=17 January 2012}}</ref> On 17 February 2012, Hall confirmed his standing as one of the premier wingers in rugby league when he scored 2 tries – including a 95-metre intercept try – and was named man-of-the-match in Leeds' 26–12 victory over Australian champions [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] in the [[2012 World Club Challenge]] played at the home venue of Leeds, [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Mascord|first=Steve|title=Rhinos trample Wolfman in his comeback game|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/rhinos-trample-wolfman-in-his-comeback-game-20120218-1tfrr.html|access-date=19 February 2012|newspaper=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]]|date=19 February 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100721195910/http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/rhinos-trample-wolfman-in-his-comeback-game-20120218-1tfrr.html|archive-date=21 July 2010|url-status=dead}}</ref> Throughout the year he showed outstanding form, which earned him the award of being named the 'World's Best Winger'.<ref>{{cite news|url= https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-ryan-hall-is-named-worlds-1395827|first=Trevor|last=Baxter|title=Great Hall's on fire: Ryan's named the game's best winger - now for the World Cup!|work=[[Daily Mirror]]|date=23 October 2012}}</ref>

He played in the [[2012 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Wembley Stadium]],<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/aug/25/leeds-warrington-challenge-cup |title=Warrington's battered Brett Hodgson recovers to see off Leeds in final|date=25 August 2012|work=Guardian UK|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19362692|title=Leeds Rhinos 18–35 Warrington Wolves|date=25 August 2012|work=BBC Sport|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref> and in the [[2012 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|title=Grand Final: Warrington 18-26 Leeds|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19805055|access-date=20 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=6 October 2012}}</ref><ref name="Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/oct/06/warrington-leeds-grand-final|title=Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington|work=The Guardian|date=6 October 2012|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref>

====2013–2018====
[[File:Ryan Hall 1.jpg|thumb|right|Hall playing for Leeds in 2015]]
[[File:Ryan Hall pre game.jpg|thumb|right|Hall pre-game for Leeds in 2016]]
In 2014, Hall signed a new five-year deal for the [[Leeds Rhinos]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Ryan Hall: Leeds Rhinos secure England wing on new contract |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/26625165.amp |website=BBC Sport |access-date=29 August 2023 |date=18 March 2014}}</ref>

He played in the [[2014 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Castleford Tigers]] at [[Wembley Stadium]],<ref name="Leeds lift Challenge Cup after Ryan Hall’s double stuns Castleford">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/aug/23/catleford-leeds-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Leeds lift Challenge Cup after Ryan Hall's double stuns Castleford|work=The Guardian|date=23 August 2014|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref> Hall scoring two tries as Leeds beat Castleford, 23–10, and was voted the winner of the [[Lance Todd Trophy]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/28884420|title=Challenge Cup final: Castleford Tigers 10-23 Leeds Rhinos|work=BBC Sport|date=23 August 2014}}</ref>

He played in the [[2015 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Leeds emphatically shut out Hull KR to lift Challenge Cup">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/aug/29/leeds-emphatically-shut-out-hull-kr-to-win-challenge-cup|title=Leeds emphatically shut out Hull KR to lift Challenge Cup|work=The Guardian|date=29 August 2015|access-date=8 November 2019}}</ref><ref name=BBC2908>{{cite web| url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/34086760 |title=Challenge Cup final: Hull KR 0-50 Leeds Rhinos |work=BBC Sport |date=29 August 2015 |access-date=29 August 2015}}</ref>

In September 2015, his 200th try for the club came against [[Huddersfield Giants]],<ref>{{cite web |title=Peacock honoured by 11th and final Dream Team selection |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/peacock-honoured-by-11th-and-final-dream-team-selection-1811960 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post |access-date=29 August 2023 |date=28 September 2015}}</ref> scoring in the final seconds of the game to clinch the [[League Leaders' Shield]] for Leeds.<ref>{{cite web |title=Ryan Hall seals Leeds top spot with last-gasp try against Huddersfield |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/sep/25/huddersfield-leeds-super-league-match-report |website=The Guardian |access-date=29 August 2023 |date=25 September 2015}}</ref> He went on to play in the [[2015 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Leeds pip Wigan to seal treble after brilliant, breathless Grand Final">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/oct/10/leeds-wigan-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title=Leeds pip Wigan to seal treble after brilliant, breathless Grand Final|work=The Guardian|date=10 October 2015|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref>

He played in the [[2017 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Castleford Tigers]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/41536394|title=Grand Final 2017: Castleford 6-24 Leeds Rhinos|date=7 October 2017|work=BBC Sport|access-date=9 October 2017}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2017/oct/07/castleford-leeds-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title=Danny McGuire guides Leeds to Grand Final success over Castleford|date=7 October 2017|work=The Guardian|access-date=9 October 2017}}</ref>

In July 2018, Hall agreed to join [[NRL]] side [[Sydney Roosters]] at the start of the 2019 season.<ref name="foxsportsau">{{cite web |title=The Roosters have signed English winger Ryan Hall as a replacement for Blake Ferguson in 2019 |url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/roosters/the-roosters-have-signed-english-winger-ryan-hall-as-a-replacement-for-blake-ferguson-in-2019/news-story/527f6b4a57e0ad5a3099e0ef1388d12a |publisher=Fox Sports Australia |access-date=23 October 2022 |date=19 July 2018}}</ref> A few weeks later, Hall played his last game for Leeds against [[Toulouse Olympique]], suffering an ACL injury which ruled him out for the rest of the season.<ref>{{cite web |title=Leeds Rhinos star Ryan Hall to miss rest of season with ACL injury |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/rugby-league-news/leeds-rhinos-ryan-hall-injury-1893668 |website=Hull Daily Mail |access-date=23 October 2022 |date=13 August 2018}}</ref>

===Sydney Roosters===
Hall signed a two-year contract with the Roosters, and was seen as a replacement on the wing for the departing [[Blake Ferguson (rugby league)|Blake Ferguson]].<ref name="foxsportsau" />

Hall made his [[NRL]] debut for the [[Sydney Roosters]] against Brisbane in Round 10 of the [[2019 NRL season]] which finished in a 15–10 loss at [[Suncorp Stadium]].  The following week, Hall made his first appearance for [[North Sydney Bears|North Sydney]], the reserve grade side for Eastern Suburbs in the [[Canterbury Cup NSW]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/roosters/rubiks-cube-in-60-secs-maths-genius-ryan-hall-is-set-to-unleash-for-chooks/news-story/849b6c601641b8cddd30cb426b1406b6/|title=Rubik's Cube in 60 secs? 'Maths genius' Ryan Hall is set to unleash for Chooks|website=Fox Sports|date=15 May 2019 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2019/05/21/canterbury-cup-nsw-team-lists--round-11/|title=Official team lists for Round 11 of the Canterbury Cup NSW|website=NSWRL}}</ref>

Hall made six appearances for the [[Sydney Roosters]] scoring no tries.  He was not included in the club's finals campaign or the grand final team which defeated Canberra to win their second successive premiership.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-grand-final-player-ratings-roosters-and-raiders-hits-and-misses-20191006-p52y53.html/|title=NRL grand final player ratings: Roosters and Raiders hits and misses|website=SMH|date=6 October 2019 }}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/49950757|title=Sydney Roosters beat Canberra Raiders to win NRL Grand Final|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

Hall made just five appearances for the [[Sydney Roosters]] in the [[2020 NRL season]] scoring no tries.  He was not included in the club's finals campaign and was subsequently released.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2020/10/09/raiders-march-on-as-three-peat-dream-ends-for-roosters/|title='More than a team': Ricky's resilient Raiders keep the dream alive|website=www.nrl.com}}</ref>

On 15 November 2020, [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] announced the signing of Hall to a two-year contract.

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
In round 1 of the 2021 Super League season, Hall made his debut for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].  After failing to score a try for two years, Hall scored a [[hat-trick]] in a 29–28 loss against [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12258696/steve-mcnamara-hails-debutant-mathieu-laguerre-after-catalans-defeat-against-hull-kr/|title=Steve McNamara hails debutant Mathieu Laguerre after Catalans defeat against Hull KR|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

In round 5 of the [[2021 Super League season]], he scored two tries in a 50–26 loss against [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/warrington-vs-hull-k-r/preview/55433/|title=Greg Inglis' debut and more Warrington Wolves vs Hull Kingston Rovers talking points|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

In round 9 of the [[2021 Super League season]], Hall scored a [[hat-trick]] in Hull KR's 40–16 victory over Leigh.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-40-leigh-16-victory-reward-for-returning-robins-fans-3255624/|title=Hull KR 40 Leigh 16 - Victory reward for returning Robins fans|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk}}</ref>
Hall made a total of 20 appearances for Hull KR in the [[2021 Super League season]] scoring 16 tries including one against the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the 2021 semi-final defeat at the [[Stade Gilbert Brutus]] He also made one appearance in the Challenge Cup.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2021/sep/30/catalans-dragons-hull-kr-super-league-play-off-semi-final-match-report-rugby-league/|title=Catalans Dragons blast past Hull KR and into their first Super League Grand Final|newspaper=The Guardian|date=30 September 2021 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/12415081/barrie-mcdermotts-2021-betfred-super-league-season-review/|title=Barrie McDermott's 2021 Betfred Super League season review|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/58663060/|title=Super League: Warrington Wolves 0-19 Hull KR - Robins set up a semi-final at Catalans|website=www.bbc.com|date=23 September 2021 }}</ref>
In round 8 of the 2023 Super League season, Hall scored a [[hat-trick]] in Hull Kingston Rovers 40–0 victory over arch-rivals [[Hull F.C.]]<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65188863|title=Super League: Hull FC 0-40 Hull KR - Robins blow rivals away|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
On 12 August, Hall played for Hull Kingston Rovers in their 17-16 golden point extra-time loss to [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]] in the Challenge Cup final.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66463438|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull KR 16-17 Leigh Leopards - Leigh claim first Wembley final win in 52 years|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
On 12 October 2024, Hall played in Hull Kingston Rovers [[2024 Super League Grand Final]] loss against Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2024/oct/12/wigan-v-hull-kr-super-league-grand-final-2024-live-score-updates?page=with:block-670aaacb8f087ed1d5a8a350/|title=Wigan v Hull KR: Super League Grand Final 2024 – as it happened|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

===Leeds Rhinos (re-join)===
On 22 April 2024 it was reported that Hall would re-join Leeds on a one-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=22 Apr 2024|accessdate=3 Oct 2024|title=Leeds re-sign winger Hall for 'final year of career'|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cqqn2pnxxnvo}}</ref> In June 2025, Hall extended his contract until the 2026 season.<ref>{{cite web |title=Super League: Ryan Hall to play on with Leeds Rhinos next season |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/czelx18jygeo |website=BBC Sport |access-date=27 January 2026 |date=3 June 2025}}</ref>

==International career==
He earned a place in the England squad for the [[2010 Four Nations]] tournament and although injury ruled him out of the first Test against New Zealand, he returned for the final game against Papua New Guinea. He also represented England in their 60–6 win over France.

Hall was also selected for the [[2011 Four Nations]], scoring 2 tries against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] at [[Wembley Stadium|Wembley]] in a 20–36 loss.

In 2013, Ryan was selected to help [[England national rugby league team|England]]'s causes in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]]. He was one of the best players in the tournament, earning himself a place on the wing in the [[RLIF]] team of the year. He scored eight tries in five matches for England. He had scored 21 tries in 20 games in total for England, which was a new record.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.express.co.uk/sport/othersport/440834/Rugby-League-Ryan-Hall-is-a-record-breaker|title=Ryan Hall is a record breaker|work=Express|date=3 November 2013}}</ref>
[[File:Ryan Hall 2013 RLWC.jpg|thumb|Hall while playing for England at the 2013 World Cup]]
In November 2014, Hall played in the [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations|2014 Four Nations]]. He was England's top try scorer in the tournament, scoring three tries. He scored his fifth try in four test matches against Australia, and he scored a double against New Zealand.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

In October 2015, Hall was picked in a 24-man England squad to play against New Zealand in a [[2015 New Zealand rugby league tour of Great Britain|three-match test-series]]. Beforehand though, England played a [http://www.sportinglife.com/rugby-league/live/match-report/43791/england-v-france test match] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151026223631/http://www.sportinglife.com/rugby-league/live/match-report/43791/england-v-france |date=26 October 2015 }} against France in Leigh. Hall scored two tries in a match where England would go on to dominate their opponents and thereon create a new record for the biggest winning margin against the 'Les Tricolores'.

The following year, Hall was picked in England's 24-man squad for the [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations|2016 Four Nations]]. On 22 October 2016, Hall extended his England try scoring record, scoring another two tries against France in Avignon. In the Four Nations, Hall scored one try in each of England's games to keep his try scoring record for England in as many appearances.

In 2017, Ryan continued his try scoring form for England in a mid-season [[2017 Pacific Rugby League Tests#England vs Samoa|test match]] against Samoa in Australia. Later in the year, he was selected as a part of England's 24-man squad for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="England rugby league World Cup squad revealed - and James Roby is back in the fold">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/james-roby-back-fold-wayne-11312505|title=England rugby league World Cup squad revealed - and James Roby is back in the fold|work=Mirror|date=9 October 2017|access-date=30 October 2019}}</ref>

He was selected in [[England 9s]] squad for the [[2019 Rugby League World Cup 9s]].<ref name="Sam Tomkins eager to lift inaugural World 9s title with England Down Under">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/sam-tomkins-eager-lift-inaugural-20595184|title=Sam Tomkins eager to lift inaugural World 9s title with England Down Under|work=Mirror|date=16 October 2019|access-date=29 October 2019}}</ref>

He was selected in squad for the [[2019 Great Britain Lions tour]] of the Southern Hemisphere.<ref name="Zak Hardaker shock inclusion in 24-man Great Britain squad for tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/14/zak-hardaker-shock-inclusion-24-man-great-britain-squad-tour/|title=Zak Hardaker shock inclusion in 24-man Great Britain squad for tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea|work=The Telegraph|date=14 October 2019|access-date=29 October 2019}}</ref> He made his [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] test debut in the defeat by Tonga.<ref name="Great Britain left bruised at Tonga party">{{cite web|url=https://www.thetimes.com/article/great-britain-tonga-rugby-league-hzmmhr5q5|title=Great Britain left bruised at Tonga party|work=Times|date=26 October 2019|access-date=29 October 2019}}</ref>

Hall was selected by England for the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]]. Hall was not included in England's opening round victory over Samoa, but was selected for the second group stage match. He scored two tries in England's 42–18 victory over France,<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63360222|title=Rugby League World Cup: England 42-18 France - Shaun Wane's side close to knockouts|work=BBC Sport }}</ref> the first of which was also his 300th career try.<ref>{{cite web |title=Ryan Hall hits 300 Career Tries |url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/61107/ryan-hall-hits-300-career-tries |publisher=The Rugby Football League |access-date=24 October 2022 |date=24 October 2022}}</ref>
In the third group stage match, Hall scored two tries in England's 94–4 victory over minnows Greece.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63439484|title=Rugby League World Cup: England crush Greece as Dom Young scores four tries|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

[[File:Ryan Hall England.jpg|thumb|Hall warming up for England in 2016]]
[[File:Ryan Hall England 2021 RLWC.jpg|thumb|Hall as part of the wider squad for England at the 2021 RLWC]]
